memory.summary: I notice a relaxing option and a quick chore, but my term project is barely started. I'll likely avoid the friction of starting now to preserve energy for later.

desires: Avoid the immediate social friction of declining an invitation to join my close friend group for lunch right now
Preserve my remaining energy reserves to ensure I don't feel exhausted or irritable when I finally engage with my teammates tomorrow

goals.description: Politely decline the lunch invitation to my close friend group (cohort_3) by citing a need for solitude to recharge, framing it as a temporary boundary rather than rejection.
goals.rationale: Aligns with 'avoidant' conflict style and high 'relationships' value; declining prevents immediate social friction that could damage long-term trust with cohort_3. Preserves energy reserves needed for future interactions.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore (quick_chore) immediately to secure a small sense of accomplishment without expending significant mental energy.
goals.rationale: Fits 'deliberate' planning horizon by clearing a minor task now to prevent future clutter, while consuming minimal resources compared to the term project. Provides a low-stakes 'win' to maintain morale.

goals.description: Engage in a brief period of leisure or rest (leisure_now) to recover physical stamina before beginning work on the term project later this afternoon.
goals.rationale: Prioritizes 'health' and 'energy conservation' to ensure I am not irritable tomorrow when working with teammates. Delaying the start of the term project aligns with my 'deliberate' nature, allowing me to approach the hard task with a clearer mind after recharging.

args.content: Hey cohort_3, I'm feeling a bit drained today and need some quiet time to recharge so I can be present with you all later. Can we skip lunch? I value our time together too much to rush it.
